event_blocks.summary: A young man in a gray Levi's t-shirt, identified as Oscar Owen, introduces a video tutorial on mobile phone magic tricks from his well-lit studio featuring acoustic panels, a microphone, and a monitor displaying 'CARD MAGIC PRO'. He explains the convenience of phone magic, emphasizing that viewers can learn tricks to amaze others with a device they always carry. The tutorial begins with a card trick demonstration on a green felt surface where a spectator thinks of a card, and a black smartphone displays an image of the 'Two of Clubs' which is then 'pulled into reality'. Owen instructs viewers to download assets from a description link to force a spectator to select a specific card, like the 'Two of Clubs', using a folded playing card and digital images on the phone. He then demonstrates how to create the illusion of pulling a red silk through the phone screen using a transparent plastic sheet with a cut-out oval, tape, and a downloadable warped home screen video, all performed on the green felt surface. The video also references a trick used to fool Jeff Bezos, shown in a vintage television frame, and features a man in a white shirt demonstrating a 'warped' phone screen illusion where apps appear skewed, achieved by a downloadable video that alters the home screen display after five seconds.

Desk Lamp.description: A modern white desk lamp with a rectangular light panel, positioned on the desk behind the young man.

Glass.description: A clear, textured glass used to pour liquid from the bottle.

Plastic Bottle.description: A clear plastic bottle with a red liquid inside, used to submerge the smartphone in a magic trick.

Monitor.description: A black monitor displaying 'CARD MAGIC PRO' in white text with a clover logo, visually setting the theme for the card magic content being discussed by the narrator.

Acoustic Panels.description: Black and white acoustic foam panels on the wall behind the young man, indicating a sound-treated room.

Jeff Bezos.description: A man in a black suit, identified by a red arrow and yellow text as 'JEFF BEZOS', watching a card trick on a vintage television screen, and mentioned by the narrator as having been 'fooled' by a magic trick.

Smartphone.description: A black smartphone with a colorful iOS home screen initially, later displaying a black-and-white patterned screen; used to perform a magic trick involving a transparent plastic sheet and red fabric, with the screen covered to create an illusion.

Microphone.description: A black microphone on a stand, positioned in front of the young man, suggesting a professional recording setup for his clear and instructional narration.

Young Man in Gray Shirt.description: A young man wearing a gray Levi's t-shirt, seated at a desk with a microphone and monitor, speaking directly to the camera. He serves as the enthusiastic and clear-voiced narrator, explaining the magic trick and its setup in an instructional tone.

Vintage Television Frame.description: A retro-style television frame with wooden casing and dials, displaying a scene of a card trick being performed for a group of men, including one labeled 'JEFF BEZOS'.

Man in White Shirt.description: A man wearing a white button-up shirt, seated at a green felt table, demonstrating interactions with a smartphone and performing a magic trick. He is the primary speaker, possessing a calm, clear, and instructional male voice, explaining the trick's mechanics and components.

Green Felt Surface.description: A textured green felt table surface, used as the consistent background for the entire sequence, providing contrast for the hands, smartphone, and red fabric.

Playing Card.description: A standard red-backed playing card, revealed to be the Two of Clubs, placed on the smartphone screen and later held up for display. Its role in a card trick and the method of its illusionary reveal are explained by an instructional male voice.

Transparent Plastic Sheet.description: A clear plastic sheet with a cut-out oval shape, used to cover the smartphone screen to create a visual illusion. The speaker describes it as having a hole, tape on three edges, and costing about $1, instructing on its placement.

Red Fabric.description: A crumpled red fabric, referred to as 'silk' by the speaker, used in the magic trick to simulate pulling an object from the smartphone, appearing to emerge from the screen and later being used to complete the illusion.

raw_segments: The video opens with a close-up of a hand performing a card trick, sliding a red-backed playing card into a smartphone's screen, which displays a patterned wallpaper, as a male narrator with a clear, slightly dramatic voice explains, "The magician pulls a card out his phone, and he pulls the silk through the middle of the screen," accompanied by dramatic, suspenseful music. The scene transitions to a man in a white shirt seated at a green table, holding a smartphone and a clear plastic bottle with red liquid, pouring the liquid into a glass before placing the phone into the bottle, which is then sealed and shaken. The narrator describes this, saying, "The phone goes inside the bottle, and the phone screen turns from being normal to being warped." The phone is then removed, seemingly unharmed, and the man displays its fully functional screen, while the narrator continues to describe various phone magic tricks, stating, "The magician visually removes the torch light from his phone multiple times. The icons fall off the screen onto the magician's hand." The video then cuts to a vintage television frame displaying a man in a black suit performing a card trick for a group of well-dressed men, including one labeled 'JEFF BEZOS' with a red arrow pointing to him, as the narrator announces, "And I'm also going to teach you the same phone trick that David Blaine used to fool Jeff Bezos. This is one of the best tricks ever, so make sure you learn how to do it," followed by a burst of male laughter. Finally, the scene shifts to a young man in a gray Levi's t-shirt, identified as Oscar Owen, sitting at a desk with a microphone and a monitor displaying 'CARD MAGIC PRO' with a clover logo. He speaks directly to the camera in a friendly, engaging tone, gesturing with his hands in a well-lit room with acoustic panels, saying, "Hey, welcome back to the channel everyone. Oscar Owen here, and in this video, I'm going to be teaching you 10 amazing magic tricks that you can do with a mobile phone. I think the wonderful thing about phone magic is you always carry your phone on you, which means even if you learn just a couple of these magic tricks..." The dramatic background music softens as he speaks.

The video opens with a Young Man in a gray Levi's t-shirt, seated at a desk, speaking directly to the camera. Behind him, a black monitor displays 'CARD MAGIC PRO' with a clover logo, flanked by a modern white desk lamp and black-and-white acoustic panels, indicating a sound-treated recording space. A black microphone stands before him. He gestures with his right hand as he enthusiastically explains, "You'll have the ability to amaze anyone you want with something that's always in your pocket." He invites viewers to "go to the card table and learn these amazing magic tricks," accompanied by a light, mysterious electronic background music. The scene then transitions to a close-up of hands performing a card trick on a green felt surface. The Young Man's voice continues, narrating the trick: "The magician asked the spectator to think of any card. They think of the Two of Clubs." A black smartphone is held, and a playing card is placed on its screen. He describes how "a card appears on the screen" and the magician "pulls that card into reality," calling it "hyper visual." The card is removed, revealing it to be the Two of Clubs, and the hands tap the phone screen to show an image of the same card, reinforcing the digital element. The card is placed back, and the screen displays a photo of the card. Text appears: 'LINK IN DESCRIPTION LEARN A CARD FORCE'. The narration then shifts to instructional details, explaining the setup: "you need to have a folded up playing card and this image and this image," referring to digital assets on the phone, and guiding viewers to download them from a link in the description. He further instructs on positioning the images and how to "force the spectator to select the card you want to produce," specifically mentioning forcing the "Two of Clubs from a different deck." The hands continue to manipulate the card and phone, demonstrating the trick's mechanics as the instructional voiceover continues.

The segment opens with a close-up of hands holding a black smartphone over a green felt surface, a small stack of red-backed playing cards resting on its initially dark screen. An instructional male voice explains how to perform a card trick, guiding the viewer to place a folded card behind the phone and then "pull the image to the left" with a thumb, creating the illusion of "pulling the card out your phone." The hands precisely manipulate the cards, sliding them across the screen before lifting one to reveal it as the Two of Clubs, as the voice concludes that the revealed card "is the card in your phone." Following a transition to a black screen with the number '2.', a man in a white button-up shirt is seen seated at the green felt table, holding the same smartphone, now displaying a colorful iOS home screen. A different, deeper male voice, with a narrative tone, identifies him as "the magician" and describes him showing his phone screen to a spectator. As the man rotates the phone, showcasing its screen and swiping through the home screen to reveal a dock, the narrative voice explains that "everything becomes warped" and "all the apps are skewed in the wrong way," calling it a "strange and fun illusion." The original instructional male voice then returns, explaining that this "easy effect" involves downloading a video where a normal home screen transitions after five seconds to the described warped display. The man's deliberate movements and the focused lighting on the phone against the consistent green felt surface emphasize the demonstration.

The video opens with a Man in White Shirt, who speaks with a calm, instructional male voice, holding a Smartphone with a colorful iOS home screen. He rotates it to display the interface, explaining, "you spin the phone like this." He continues, "And you see your timer like that and the phone screen looks as if it's now warped," as he transitions to a magic trick, placing the phone screen-down and revealing a Red Fabric that appears to emerge from the device. Throughout this, a calm, slightly mysterious instrumental background music begins to play. The scene shifts to a close-up of his hands manipulating a Transparent Plastic Sheet with a cut-out oval shape. The man's voice narrates, "The magician slowly and cleanly pulls the silk through the phone. You can learn this trick in less than 30 seconds. Here is how." He then details the trick's components: "This magic trick requires some silk, a phone, and this little bit of plastic here that costs about, you know, $1 off the internet. I'll leave a link to where you can buy some." He instructs, "Get the plastic and you can see it's got a hole going through the middle of it and I put some tape on three of the edges." The sheet is then placed over the phone’s screen, with an animated overlay illustrating its placement and alignment with the phone’s notch. He continues, "Then what you're going to do is you can download this image from the description as well. Place this onto your phone screen." The man demonstrates how the sheet creates an illusion by covering the screen, explaining, "And as you can see, this really hides the um the plastic, the illusion behind it. So it looks like there's nothing on your screen." He then uses the Red Fabric to simulate pulling something from the phone, reinforcing the trick. The video concludes with the man placing the Transparent Plastic Sheet over the phone and using the Red Fabric to complete the illusion, all set against the consistent Green Felt Surface, as he states, "To make things easier, I'll turn off that image."

event_blocks.summary: Hands on a green felt surface peel a transparent plastic sheet from a black smartphone while subtle, upbeat, and slightly mysterious background music plays, and a male voice explains a magic trick, referring to a red fabric as "silk" and the plastic sheet as having a "hole." The hands use the red fabric to wipe the phone, revealing a cracked screen, then rotate it to show the iOS home screen with the time 12:50. The hands place the transparent plastic sheet with a cut-out over the phone, aligning it with the notch, and use the red fabric to simulate pulling an object from the screen, creating the illusion that the silk is being pulled right through the phone. The scene transitions to a young man in a gray Levi's t-shirt, seated at a desk with a microphone and a monitor displaying 'CARD MAGIC PRO', speaking enthusiastically to the camera about his book's release in the USA at the end of the month, with a later release elsewhere due to "COVID and shipping issues." A book cover titled 'MIND-BLOWING MAGIC TRICKS FOR EVERYONE' by Oscar Owen, featuring hands fanning playing cards, is shown, and the young man's voice explains the book teaches "over 50 amazing magic tricks with cards, coins, everyday objects and so much more." The book is then shown open with step-by-step instructions for a card trick. The young man reappears, describing the book's "powerful and visual" tricks that are "guaranteed to amaze." A close-up shows hands cutting an orange on a plate to reveal a playing card inside, demonstrating a trick. The final shot is the book cover with the text '50+ INCREDIBLE TRICKS INSIDE' appearing, as the young man mentions it took him "well over a year to write the book" before his speech is cut off.

Desk Lamp.description: A modern white desk lamp with a rectangular light panel, positioned on the desk behind the young man.

Book Cover.description: A black book cover titled 'MIND-BLOWING MAGIC TRICKS FOR EVERYONE' by Oscar Owen, featuring a black-and-white image of hands fanning playing cards, with text indicating '50 CARD, COIN AND MENTALISM TRICKS USING EVERYDAY OBJECTS', and later displaying an overlay '50+ INCREDIBLE TRICKS INSIDE'; described by the young man as containing 'over 50 amazing magic tricks with cards, coins, everyday objects and so much more'.

Orange.description: A whole orange being cut in half on a white plate with a black rim, revealing a playing card inside one half, used as part of a magic trick demonstration, which the young man describes as a 'powerful and visual' trick.

Transparent Plastic Sheet.description: A clear plastic sheet with a cut-out oval shape, used to cover the smartphone screen to create a visual illusion, shown being manipulated and aligned with the phone’s notch, and referred to by a male voice as having a 'hole' through which the 'silk' is threaded.

Red Fabric.description: A crumpled red fabric, used in the magic trick to simulate pulling an object from the smartphone, appearing to emerge from the screen and later being used to complete the illusion; referred to as 'silk' by a male voice explaining the trick.

Smartphone.description: A black smartphone with a colorful iOS home screen initially, later displaying a black-and-white patterned screen; used to perform a magic trick involving a transparent plastic sheet and red fabric, with the screen covered to create an illusion, as a male voice explains how the 'silk' is pulled 'right through the phone'.

Green Felt Surface.description: A textured green felt table surface, used as the consistent background for the entire sequence, providing contrast for the hands, smartphone, and red fabric.

Man in White Shirt.description: A man wearing a white button-up shirt, seated at a green felt table, demonstrating interactions with a smartphone by holding it, rotating it, and swiping through the home screen, then performing a magic trick using a red fabric and a transparent plastic sheet, while a male voice (likely his) explains the illusion, referring to the red fabric as 'silk' and the plastic sheet as having a 'hole'.

Monitor.description: A black monitor displaying 'CARD MAGIC PRO' in white text with a clover logo, positioned behind the young man speaking to the camera.

Acoustic Panels.description: Black and white acoustic foam panels on the wall behind the young man, indicating a sound-treated room.

Microphone.description: A black microphone on a stand, positioned in front of the young man as he speaks.

Young Man in Gray Shirt.description: A young man wearing a gray Levi's t-shirt, seated at a desk with a microphone and monitor, speaking directly to the camera in a clear, enthusiastic male voice about his magic book, mentioning its release dates and the types of 'powerful and visual' tricks it contains.

Book Cover Text Overlay.description: A text overlay on the right side of the screen reading '50+ INCREDIBLE TRICKS INSIDE' in bold yellow font, appearing alongside the book cover image.

Open Book Pages.description: Pages from the magic book showing step-by-step instructions for a card trick, including numbered steps, images of hands performing the trick, and text explanations, with a QR code visible in the top left corner, as the young man describes the book's contents.

raw_segments: The video opens with a close-up of hands on a green felt surface, peeling a transparent plastic sheet from a black smartphone. Throughout this segment, a subtle, upbeat, and slightly mysterious background music plays. A male voice, likely belonging to the demonstrator, explains the magic trick, referring to the red fabric as "silk" and the transparent plastic sheet as having a "hole." The hands then use the red fabric to wipe the phone, revealing a cracked screen. The phone is rotated, and its screen lights up to show the iOS home screen with the time 12:50. The hands then place the transparent plastic sheet with a cut-out over the phone, aligning it with the notch, and use the red fabric to simulate pulling an object from the screen, creating a visual illusion, as the voice explains, "you're pulling the silk right through the phone." The scene transitions to a young man in a gray Levi's t-shirt, seated at a desk with a microphone and a monitor displaying 'CARD MAGIC PRO'. He speaks directly to the camera in a clear, enthusiastic male voice, introducing his book and mentioning its release in the USA at the end of the month, with a slightly later release elsewhere due to "COVID and shipping issues." The video cuts to a book cover titled 'MIND-BLOWING MAGIC TRICKS FOR EVERYONE' by Oscar Owen, featuring a black-and-white image of hands fanning playing cards. The young man's voice continues, explaining that the book teaches "over 50 amazing magic tricks with cards, coins, everyday objects and so much more." The book is then shown open, displaying step-by-step instructions for a card trick with images and text. The video returns to the young man, who continues speaking about the book's "powerful and visual" tricks that are "guaranteed to amaze." This is followed by a cut to a close-up of hands cutting an orange on a plate, revealing a playing card inside, demonstrating one such trick. The final shot shows the book cover again, this time with the text '50+ INCREDIBLE TRICKS INSIDE' appearing on the right side of the screen, as the young man concludes by mentioning it took him "well over a year to write the book" before his speech is cut off.

event_blocks.summary: A Young Man in Gray Shirt, seated before a Monitor displaying 'CARD MAGIC PRO' and a Microphone, promotes a pre-order for the software at approximately $12, noting its early arrival before Christmas and its value for learning magic tricks, directing viewers to the first link in the description as a channel support. Subsequently, a Man in White Shirt, positioned at a Green Felt Surface, demonstrates a card trick. He places a Smartphone with a prediction onto the table, shuffles and cuts a deck of red-backed Playing Cards, and instructs a hypothetical spectator to stop dealing at any point for a free choice. The magician then uses his phone to mark the chosen card, revealing it to be the Queen of Diamonds, and shows the phone displaying a red-backed card before placing it over a physical card to reveal the Queen of Diamonds. Further elaborating on the trick, the Man in White Shirt holds the Queen of Diamonds, photographs it, then flips it to the Ten of Spades and photographs that as well, explaining the effect involves playing cards and a phone. He instructs viewers to photograph their chosen card with live view on an iPhone, then sets a photo of a red-backed card as the lock screen wallpaper. After placing the phone face-down, he shuffles red-backed cards, places the phone on the deck, and positions the photographed card face-down behind the phone, subsequently placing the phone and card on the table's edge. He picks up the phone, reveals the red-backed card image on the lock screen, places it down, then picks it up again, explaining the spectator can shuffle and deal cards freely.

Desk Lamp.description: A modern white desk lamp with a rectangular light panel, positioned on the desk behind the young man.

Monitor.description: A black monitor displaying 'CARD MAGIC PRO' in white text with a clover logo, positioned behind the young man speaking to the camera, which he refers to as a product that teaches magic tricks.

Acoustic Panels.description: Black and white acoustic foam panels on the wall behind the young man, indicating a sound-treated room.

Green Felt Surface.description: A textured green felt table surface, used as the consistent background for the entire sequence, providing contrast for the hands, smartphone, and red-backed cards.

Playing Card.description: A standard red-backed playing card, initially shown as the Queen of Diamonds, then flipped to reveal the Ten of Spades. The speaker refers to it as 'the card you want to reveal' and 'the card you just photographed,' which is then used as a lock screen wallpaper on the smartphone and as part of the physical card deck.

Smartphone.description: A black smartphone, identified by the speaker as potentially an 'iPhone,' initially displaying a colorful iOS home screen, later a black-and-white patterned screen. It is used to photograph playing cards and set a red-backed card as the lock screen wallpaper, then placed on a deck of cards as part of the trick.

Microphone.description: A black microphone on a stand, positioned in front of the young man as he speaks.

Young Man in Gray Shirt.description: A young man wearing a gray Levi's t-shirt, seated at a desk with a microphone and monitor, speaking directly to the camera in a well-lit room with acoustic panels and a desk lamp. He introduces 'Card Magic Pro' as a pre-order for $12, arriving before Christmas, and encourages viewers to purchase it to support the channel and learn amazing magic tricks.

Man in White Shirt.description: A man wearing a white button-up shirt, seated at a green felt table, demonstrating interactions with a smartphone by holding it, rotating it, and swiping through the home screen, then performing a magic trick using a deck of red-backed playing cards. He provides a calm, instructional male voice-over throughout the segment, explaining the steps of the trick.

raw_segments: The video opens with a Young Man in Gray Shirt, seated at a desk in a well-lit room with acoustic panels and a desk lamp, speaking directly to the camera. Behind him, a Monitor displays 'CARD MAGIC PRO' with a clover logo, and a Microphone is positioned in front of him. He gestures as he explains that 'Card Magic Pro' is a really cheap pre-order at about $12, arriving well before Christmas, encouraging viewers to check it out via the first link in the description, noting it supports the channel and teaches amazing magic tricks. The scene then transitions to a different setting where a Man in White Shirt, narrating his own actions as 'the magician,' is seated at a Green Felt Surface, performing a card trick. He places a Smartphone on the table, stating it has a prediction on it, then shuffles and cuts a deck of red-backed Playing Cards. He instructs a hypothetical spectator to deal down cards and stop whenever they like, emphasizing it's a free choice. After the spectator stops, the magician uses his phone to mark the chosen card, preventing any changes. He reminds the spectator they could have stopped at any other card, but the one they stopped at was the Queen of Diamonds. He then picks up the phone, which now displays a red-backed card, and places it on top of a physical card, revealing the Queen of Diamonds, just as he begins to press his home button. The entire sequence is performed with precise hand movements, emphasizing the magic trick’s mechanics and the interaction between the physical cards and the smartphone, all while the magician's voice guides the viewer through each step.

A Man in White Shirt, seated at a Green Felt Surface, demonstrates a magic trick involving a Smartphone and playing cards, while providing a calm, instructional voice-over. He begins by holding a Playing Card (Queen of Diamonds), which he places on the table and photographs with his phone, then flips it to the Ten of Spades and photographs that too. The voice explains this is "a brilliant effect that uses playing cards and a phone," guiding the viewer to "take the card that you want to reveal, turn it face down," and if using an iPhone, "photograph it making sure live view is on." He then navigates the phone's settings, following the voice's instruction to "turn this into your wallpaper" by selecting a photo of a red-backed card to set as the lock screen. After placing the phone face-down, he shuffles a deck of red-backed cards, then places the phone on top of the deck. The voice instructs to "take the card you just photographed and place it face down behind the phone like this," and to "take the phone and the card and place them on the edge of the table." He picks up the phone, revealing the red-backed card image on the lock screen, then places it back down before picking it up again, completing the setup for the magic trick as the voice explains how to "shuffle the cards and then let the spectator deal down as many cards as they like."

event_blocks.summary: A Man in White Shirt, seated at a Green Felt Surface, demonstrates a card trick involving a Smartphone and Playing Cards. He places the Smartphone on a stack of red-backed Playing Cards, stating, "we're gonna leave this here so I can't change your card and we're gonna mark it using the phone." He then photographs the Ten of Spades with the Smartphone, setting it as the lock screen wallpaper, and explains he has "deposited the extra card on top of their pile." After revealing the Queen of Diamonds, he places the phone back on the deck, instructing to "distract them from this part for a couple of seconds" before picking up the pile and presenting the Ten of Spades. He performs a "force press" on the home screen, showing "the card flips over and it's the Ten of Spades. And that's how the magic trick works." The scene shifts to a black screen with a '5.' and subtle music, after which the Man in White Shirt holds the Smartphone in one hand and a collection of Miniature App Icons, seemingly pulled from the phone’s screen, in the other. He explains, "The icons fall off the screen and into the magician's hands. This is super visual." Gesturing with the phone and swiping the home screen, he maintains the illusion of physically extracting apps, with focused lighting on his hands and the phone against a dark background. He concludes by holding the phone and icons, beginning to explain the trick's preparation: "Firstly, cut out all these little icons... and then also download this video which is 5 seconds of normal screen and then 5 seconds where the screen turns blank."

Playing Card.description: A standard red-backed playing card, initially shown as the Queen of Diamonds, then flipped to reveal the Ten of Spades. The speaker explicitly identifies it as 'the card you selected, the Ten of Spades,' which is photographed and later set as the lock screen wallpaper on the smartphone.

Smartphone.description: A black smartphone, initially showing a colorful iOS home screen and later a black-and-white patterned screen. It is used to photograph a playing card, set it as a lock screen wallpaper, and is central to the magic trick where miniature app icons appear to be physically extracted from its screen. The speaker describes actions like 'force press' on the home screen and mentions a video with '5 seconds of normal screen and then 5 seconds where the screen turns blank' related to the phone's display.

Miniature App Icons.description: Small, colorful, physical representations of smartphone app icons (e.g., Messages, Photos, Spotify) that appear in the man’s palm, seemingly extracted from the smartphone screen during the magic trick. The speaker describes them as 'icons fall off the screen' and instructs viewers to 'cut out all these little icons' to perform the trick.

Green Felt Surface.description: A textured green felt table surface, used as the consistent background for the entire sequence, providing contrast for the hands, smartphone, and red-backed cards.

Man in White Shirt.description: A man wearing a white button-up shirt, seated at a green felt table, demonstrating interactions with a smartphone and performing magic tricks. He is the speaker, providing a clear, instructional male voice, explaining the steps of the card trick and later how to perform the 'super visual' app icon trick, referring to himself as 'the magician'.

raw_segments: The video opens with a Man in White Shirt seated at a Green Felt Surface, performing a card trick. He places a Smartphone on a small stack of red-backed Playing Cards, explaining in a clear male voice, "we're gonna leave this here so I can't change your card and we're gonna mark it using the phone." He then picks up the phone and uses it to photograph a card, the Ten of Spades, which he sets as the lock screen wallpaper, while continuing to explain that he has "deposited the extra card on top of their pile." He proceeds to flip through the cards, revealing the Queen of Diamonds, and then places the phone back on the deck, instructing to "distract them from this part for a couple of seconds" and then to "pick up the pile and say here's the card you selected, the Ten of Spades." He demonstrates a "force press" on the home screen, noting "the card flips over and it's the Ten of Spades. And that's how the magic trick works." The scene transitions to a black screen with the number '5.' displayed, accompanied by subtle background music. The man then holds the Smartphone in one hand, revealing a collection of Miniature App Icons in his other palm, seemingly pulled from the phone’s screen. The male voice explains, "The icons fall off the screen and into the magician's hands. This is super visual." He gestures with the phone, swiping through the home screen, while the tiny icons remain in his hand, creating a visual illusion of apps being physically extracted. The lighting is focused on his hands and the phone, with a dark background emphasizing the trick’s details. The sequence ends with him holding the phone and the icons, maintaining the illusion, as he begins to explain how to perform the trick: "Firstly, cut out all these little icons... and then also download this video which is 5 seconds of normal screen and then 5 seconds where the screen turns blank."

event_blocks.summary: A Man in White Shirt demonstrates a magic trick, initially showing a Smartphone with its iOS home screen and miniature App Icons, explaining how to create the illusion of icons appearing in his palm. The scene shifts to a Vintage Television Frame showing Jeff Bezos and others watching a card trick, with the narrator promising to teach an effect that fooled Bezos. Returning to the Man in White Shirt at a Green Felt Surface, he shuffles red-backed Playing Cards and explains a trick involving a phone and cards, instructing a spectator to freely choose five cards. He places three cards down, uses the Smartphone's calculator app to perform a calculation (453 + 314 * 1000 + 410 = 768,410), and reveals five face-down cards, which are then turned over to match the calculated digits: 7 of Clubs, 6 of Hearts, 8 of Spades, 4 of Clubs, and 10 of Diamonds. He resets the calculator for another sequence (405 + 100 * 4 = 435,810) and instructs on how to force cards from a deck to match a 'force number' derived from calculator input, detailing techniques like using a pinky break to control selected cards. The demonstration emphasizes precise hand movements, intimate lighting focused on the hands and objects, and a consistent background track.

Playing Card.description: A standard deck of red-backed playing cards, initially fanned to reveal specific face cards (7 of Clubs, 6 of Hearts, 8 of Spades, 4 of Clubs, 10 of Diamonds), then shuffled and manipulated with skilled hand movements. A male voice provides detailed instructions on how to 'force all of these cards in this order on the spectator,' describing specific manipulations like turning cards over, placing them at the bottom, maintaining a 'pinky break,' and having a spectator touch five cards. The voice also describes twisting and collecting these cards as part of the trick.

Jeff Bezos.description: A man in a black suit, identified by a red arrow and yellow text as 'JEFF BEZOS', seen watching a card trick on a vintage television screen, and explicitly named by the narrator as someone David Blaine fooled with the trick being taught.

Smartphone.description: A black smartphone with a calculator app displayed, initially showing '0', then placed face-down on the green felt surface with the screen off. A male voice refers to it as 'the calculator' and instructs to 'leave it to the side' as part of a magic trick setup involving a 'force number'.

Miniature App Icons.description: Small, colorful, physical representations of smartphone app icons appearing in the man’s palm, described by the narrator as disappearing from the virtual world and moving into the physical world during the magic trick.

Green Felt Surface.description: A textured green felt table surface, serving as the consistent background for the entire sequence, providing contrast for the hands, smartphone, and red-backed cards.

Vintage Television Frame.description: A retro-style television frame with wooden casing and dials, displaying a scene of a card trick being performed for a group of men, including one labeled 'JEFF BEZOS'.

Man in White Shirt.description: A man wearing a white button-up shirt, seated at a green felt table, demonstrating a mathematical card trick by using a smartphone calculator and a set of playing cards. A male narrator, likely the man himself, provides detailed instructions and explanations for the trick, guiding the viewer through calculations and card reveals.

raw_segments: The segment opens with a close-up of the Man in White Shirt demonstrating a magic trick, rotating his Smartphone to display its colorful iOS home screen before swiping through it. As he performs, his clear, instructional male voice explains how to make miniature App Icons appear in his palm, seemingly extracted from the phone, detailing the illusion of icons moving from the virtual to the physical world. The scene transitions to a black screen with white text reading '6. (My Favourite Trick!)', followed by a Vintage Television Frame showing a group of men, including one labeled 'JEFF BEZOS', watching a card trick. The narrator's voice returns, announcing he will teach the same effect that David Blaine used to "fool Jeff Bezos," promising a full performance and a tutorial. The video then cuts back to the Man in White Shirt, now seated at a Green Felt Surface, shuffling a deck of red-backed Playing Cards. His voice continues, describing the setup of a card trick where "the magician has a phone and a deck," instructing a spectator to touch the back of "five random cards" as a "genuinely free choice." He places three cards face-down on the table, picks up the Smartphone, and opens the calculator app, tapping the screen as the narrator explains the magician leaving the spectator's selection sticking out, pulling them out, and counting out all five cards. The dramatic, intimate lighting focuses on his hands and the table throughout the demonstration, accompanied by a consistent background track.

The video opens with a close-up of a Man in White Shirt seated at a Green Felt Surface, holding a Smartphone displaying a calculator app. A male narrator begins explaining the trick, instructing a spectator to type any three-digit number, then multiply it by another three-digit number, and then by another one, to create a very random number that hopefully matches chosen cards. Visually, the man rapidly inputs numbers, starting with 453, adding 314 to reach 767, multiplying by 1000 to arrive at 767,000, and finally adding 410 to display 768,410. To the right of the phone, three face-down Playing Cards are laid out. The narrator states that the cards are then turned over, and the man flips them one by one, revealing the 7 of Clubs, 6 of Hearts, 8 of Spades, 4 of Clubs, and 10 of Diamonds, with the narrator explicitly naming "a seven, then a six, then an eight, then a four, then a ten" as they appear, confirming they match the calculated 768,410. He gathers the cards, shuffles them briefly, and places them face down. The camera angle shifts slightly as he resets the calculator to 0, then begins a new calculation: entering 405, adding 100, and multiplying by 4 to get 435,810. The cards remain on the table, and a deck of red-backed cards is visible to the right. The narrator then instructs to "go into your deck and remove the following cards: a four, a three, a five, an eight, and a ten," and to "open up your calculator app and type in those numbers," followed by adding zero and multiplying by zero, explaining that this means it doesn't matter what you add or multiply by, as long as you multiply all these numbers and press equals, it's always going to... The lighting is focused on the hands and objects, with a dark background emphasizing the green felt surface, all set to a subtle background music.

The segment opens with a close-up of a hand holding a black smartphone displaying a calculator app with a '0' on a green felt surface. A male voice immediately begins speaking, stating "435810" and explaining, "So this is a force number." He then instructs, "Now this is assessing the calculator, you leave it to the side," as the hand places the phone down, its screen now off, and picks up a deck of red-backed playing cards. The voice continues, detailing how to "force all of these cards in this order on the spectator," while the hand skillfully fans the cards to reveal the 7 of Clubs, 6 of Hearts, 8 of Spades, 4 of Clubs, and 10 of Diamonds. The speaker elaborates on different methods, explaining his approach: "I turned all the cards over so the four's in the top, the ten is on the bottom, and you place all of these cards on the bottom of the deck and you keep a pinky break." Concurrently, the hand squares and shuffles the cards with precise riffle shuffles and cuts, demonstrating practiced dexterity. The voice then instructs, "Then let the spectator touch the back of five cards," counting "one, two, three, four, and that's five," while the hand continues to manipulate the deck, fanning and squaring with fluidity. The speaker reiterates, "And you're keeping your pinky break under under the cards that you just selected and you got these five cards sticking out." The hand twists the cards to the side as instructed, and the voice explains, "You've got a pinky break under this these cards here and you've got these five cards sticking to the side." The segment concludes with the voice instructing to "collect these cards and as you do, bring these cards underneath them," as the hand continues its intricate card handling, with the smartphone remaining visible in the lower right corner on the consistent green felt backdrop.

event_blocks.summary: A Man in White Shirt, performing as a magician on a Green Felt Surface, expertly shuffles and fans red-backed Playing Cards, revealing the 7 of Clubs, 6 of Hearts, 8 of Spades, 4 of Clubs, and 10 of Diamonds while explaining a card trick, specifically how to "strip them out" and "force the spectator these cards." He then places the cards face-down, picks up a Smartphone displaying a calculator app, and inputs numbers, stating the spectator will "multiply any numbers together" to arrive at the result "435810," which is shown on the phone. The Man in White Shirt then arranges three cards face-up, counting "4, 3, 5, 8, 10" to confirm the trick's outcome. The scene shifts to a black screen with the number '7.' before a narrator announces, "The magician has a phone in a bottle." The Man in White Shirt is then seen placing the Smartphone into a clear Plastic Bottle, which he seals with a cap. The narrator confirms, "He pushes the phone right inside the bottle. The phone is stuck inside." The phone's screen, displaying the time '09:55' and date 'Saturday 21 November', remains active and updates to '09:56' after the bottle is gently shaken, demonstrating the phone's continued functionality while inside. Background music accompanies the demonstrations throughout the segment.

Plastic Bottle.description: A clear plastic bottle, initially empty, used to submerge the smartphone. A narrator refers to it as 'a bottle' that the phone is 'right inside' and 'stuck inside' after being sealed with a cap.

Playing Card.description: A standard deck of red-backed playing cards, initially fanned to reveal specific face cards (7 of Clubs, 6 of Hearts, 8 of Spades, 4 of Clubs, 10 of Diamonds), then shuffled and manipulated with skilled hand movements. The Man in White Shirt refers to 'five cards the spectator chose' and 'your five cards underneath,' and later counts out '4, 3, 5, 8, 10' while arranging three cards face-up on the table, implying these are the revealed cards.

Smartphone.description: A black smartphone with a calculator app displayed, initially showing '0', then displaying '435,810' after input, which the Man in White Shirt explicitly states as the trick's outcome. Later, it shows the time '09:55' and date 'Saturday 21 November' while submerged in a clear plastic bottle, updating to '09:56' after a gentle shake, confirming its functionality. A narrator refers to it as 'a phone in a bottle' that is 'stuck inside.'

Green Felt Surface.description: A textured green felt table surface, serving as the consistent background for the entire sequence, providing contrast for the hands, smartphone, and red-backed cards.

Man in White Shirt.description: A man wearing a white button-up shirt, seated at a green felt table, demonstrating a mathematical card trick by using a smartphone calculator and a set of playing cards, then performing a magic trick by placing the smartphone into a plastic bottle. He speaks throughout the first part, explaining the card trick, using terms like 'strip them out,' 'force the spectator,' and explicitly stating numbers '435810' and '4, 3, 5, 8, 10.' He is also referred to as 'the magician' by a narrator.

raw_segments: The segment opens with the Man in White Shirt, identified as a magician, skillfully manipulating a deck of red-backed Playing Cards on a Green Felt Surface. He shuffles and fans the cards, revealing specific face cards including the 7 of Clubs, 6 of Hearts, 8 of Spades, 4 of Clubs, and 10 of Diamonds, while audibly explaining a card trick, detailing how to "strip them out" and "force the spectator these cards." He then places the cards face-down and picks up a Smartphone, which displays a calculator app. As he inputs a series of numbers, he explains that the spectator will "multiply any numbers together" and the result will be "435810," which is then displayed on the phone. He places the phone down and arranges three cards face-up on the table, counting out "4, 3, 5, 8, 10" as he does so, confirming the outcome of the trick. The scene transitions to a black screen with the number '7.' in white text. A narrator's voice then describes the next act, stating "The magician has a phone in a bottle." The Man in White Shirt is seen holding the Smartphone and a clear Plastic Bottle, and he proceeds to place the phone into the bottle, which is then sealed with a cap. The narrator confirms, "He pushes the phone right inside the bottle. The phone is stuck inside." The phone’s screen remains on, showing the time '09:55' and date 'Saturday 21 November', and updates to '09:56' after he gently shakes the bottle, demonstrating its continued functionality while submerged. Throughout the segment, background music plays, accompanying the magic demonstration.

event_blocks.summary: A man in a white shirt, acting as a magician, sits at a green felt table while a narrator explains a trick. The magician holds a smartphone and a clear plastic bottle, placing the phone down and then picking it up to reveal its screen displaying '09:55' and 'Saturday 21 November'. Following instructions, he cuts a slit in the bottle with a knife and pushes the smartphone through it, demonstrating that it is inside. The narrator explains the illusion of cutting the bottle to remove the phone, which the magician then successfully extracts, appearing undamaged, and places back on the table. After a brief transition to a black screen with a white '8' and a swell in background music, the magician is shown holding the smartphone, now displaying a calculator app, and a small clear glass filled with water. The narrator describes the trick as having virtual water on the phone, and the magician tilts the phone over the glass, causing a single drop of water to fall from the screen into the glass, creating the illusion of the phone leaking water against the dark background and green felt surface.

Glass.description: A small, clear, textured glass filled with water, placed on a black mat, into which a single drop of 'virtual water' appears to fall from the smartphone’s screen as part of a magic trick.

Plastic Bottle.description: A clear, empty plastic bottle with a narrow neck and a white cap, revealed by the narrator to have a 'giant slit' cut into its side, used to secretly enclose the smartphone while allowing its screen to remain visible and functional.

Smartphone.description: A black smartphone with a glossy finish, initially displaying the time '09:55' and date 'Saturday 21 November', later showing a calculator app with a blue and white interface, and appearing to drip 'virtual water' from its screen as part of a magic trick.

Green Felt Surface.description: A textured green felt table surface, serving as the primary background for the entire sequence, providing contrast for the hands, smartphone, and glass.

Man in White Shirt.description: A man wearing a white button-up shirt, seated at a green felt table, identified by the narrator as the 'magician' who demonstrates a magic trick by placing a smartphone into a plastic bottle via a hidden slit and later showing it 'leaking' 'virtual water' into a glass.

raw_segments: The video opens with a male narrator explaining "the secret behind this effect" as a man in a white shirt, identified as the magician, sits at a green felt table. The narrator instructs to "cut a giant slit in the side of the bottle with a knife," which the magician demonstrates by holding a smartphone and a clear plastic bottle. He places the phone down, then picks it up, revealing its screen displaying '09:55' and 'Saturday 21 November'. Following the narrator's guidance to "push the phone through the slit" and "show that it's really in there," the magician carefully inserts the smartphone into the bottle, ensuring the screen remains visible and functional. The narrator continues, explaining how to "pretend to cut into the bottle" with a knife to remove the phone, when in reality, it's just going through the existing slit. The magician then removes the phone, which appears undamaged, and places it back on the table. A brief transition to a black screen with a white number '8' occurs, accompanied by a slight swell in the background music. The scene then shifts to the magician holding the smartphone, now displaying a calculator app, and a small clear glass filled with water. The narrator introduces this as a trick where "the magician has a glass of water and some virtual water on his phone," as the magician tilts the phone over the glass, and a single drop of water falls from the screen into the glass, creating a visual illusion of the phone leaking water, all set against a dark background emphasizing the actions on the green felt surface.

event_blocks.summary: The Man in White Shirt, seated at a green felt surface, initiates a series of smartphone-based illusions, beginning with a black Smartphone placed screen-down over a clear Glass, creating a 'virtual water' effect accompanied by a 'swish' sound, which he admits is impractical but fun. He then demonstrates the trick, advising viewers to use a water simulator app and a glass that fits behind the phone, creating the illusion of pouring water from the phone into the glass, accompanied by a 'clink' sound. Transitioning to a 'BONUS TRICK' segment, he uses a gold Smartphone and a glowing object to create a 'phone torch' effect, calling it the 'ultimate party trick' that works well in the dark, displaying a control center interface on the phone's screen. He then demonstrates another trick using a beige Finger Sleeve on his right index finger, interacting with a black smartphone displaying a control center interface, explaining how the thumb tip creates a light when pressed. He taps the phone screen, turning the torch on and off, creating the illusion of taking and throwing the light, which he describes as a simple, easy, and amazing effect, concluding with a call to subscribe and check out the book below.

Glass.description: A small, clear, textured glass, initially filled with water, used to receive a drop of water seemingly from the smartphone’s screen, accompanied by a "swish" sound. Later shown empty and used again in a trick where the phone is placed inside, with a "clink" sound, maintaining the illusion of emptiness. The narrator refers to it as a "glass of water that can fit behind your phone."

Smartphone.description: A black smartphone with a glossy finish, initially displaying a blue abstract wallpaper, used in a water-dripping illusion. The male narrator explains that this trick involves a "water simulator app" and careful positioning to create the illusion of water pouring from the phone.

Gold Smartphone.description: A gold-colored smartphone with a glossy finish, held by the Man in White Shirt, displaying a control center interface with app icons, whose 'torch' (flashlight) is used in conjunction with the finger sleeve to create a light-based illusion, as explained by the narrator.

Green Felt Surface.description: A textured green felt table surface, serving as the primary background for the entire sequence, providing contrast for the hands, smartphone, and finger sleeve.

Man in White Shirt.description: A man wearing a white button-up shirt, seated at a green felt table in a dimly lit setting, demonstrating a magic trick with a gold smartphone and a beige finger sleeve (referred to as a 'thumb tip' by the narrator), which glows red as if reacting to the phone's 'torch' (flashlight) being turned on and off, creating an illusion of 'taking a light and then throwing it back on.'

Bonus Trick Text Overlay.description: White text on a black background reading '9. (BONUS TRICK)', signaling a transition to a new segment of the video.

Text Overlay.description: White text on a black background reading 'PLEASE SUBSCRIBE CHECK OUT THE BOOK BELOW', appearing at the end of the video as a call to action for viewers.

Finger Sleeve.description: A beige, flexible finger sleeve, referred to by the narrator as a 'thumb tip' that costs 'like £3 or something,' worn on the index finger, used to interact with the smartphone screen and glow red when tapped, creating a digital or light-based illusion by simulating the phone's 'torch' being taken and thrown.

raw_segments: The minute begins with a Man in White Shirt, seated at a green felt table, holding a black Smartphone and a clear Glass filled with water. He places the phone screen-down over the glass, and a single drop of water appears to fall from the phone’s screen into the glass, creating a visual illusion accompanied by a subtle "swish" sound. A male voice, belonging to the man, explains this "virtual water" trick, noting it's "quite an impractical effect, but I thought it was a bit of fun." The scene shifts to a close-up of the table, where the phone is placed screen-up, displaying a blue abstract wallpaper, as the man's hand gestures toward it. The narrator details the trick, advising viewers to "get a water simulator app" and "let half the water run out" before positioning a "glass of water that can fit behind your phone." He then picks up the phone, flips it to show the back, and proceeds to demonstrate a trick by placing the phone into a clear, now empty Glass, then removing it, revealing the glass is still empty, with a distinct "clink" sound. He repeats the action with a second glass, maintaining the illusion, while the narrator explains that "from the front point of view, it creates the illusion... that you're actually pouring water from the phone into the cup." The scene transitions to a black screen with white text reading '9. (BONUS TRICK)', accompanied by continuous background music. The man reappears, now holding a gold-colored Smartphone, and performs a new trick by placing a small, glowing object near the phone, which then appears to emit light from the screen. The narrator describes this as a "phone torch" trick, calling it "the ultimate party trick" that "works really well at night or when it's dark." He places the phone on the table, and the screen displays a control center interface with various app icons, suggesting a digital interaction or illusion, with the narrator concluding it's "a really fun magic trick to do at parties or nighttime because it..." as the audio fades. The lighting throughout is dim, focusing attention on the phone and the man’s hands, emphasizing the magical effect.

The video opens with a close-up of a person's hands on a green felt surface, where their right index finger, wrapped in a beige finger sleeve, interacts with a black smartphone displaying a control center interface. A male narrator begins by explaining the trick "uses the medium of light," instructing viewers to "get a thumb tip like this," noting they "cost like £3 or something," and that "when you press the end of the thumb tip, a light appears." The person adjusts the finger sleeve, taps the phone screen, which briefly turns off then back on, showing a battery icon. The narrator continues, detailing how to "go on your phone, turn on the torch," and "put the torch on like this." The person then uses the finger sleeve to tap the screen again, causing a glowing red light to appear on its tip, demonstrating the illusion. The narrator further explains the illusion, stating, "you pretend to take the torch and as soon as you do that, you turn the torch off," and then "pretend to throw it back on, off, on." The scene transitions to a wider shot of the Man in White Shirt, seated at the green felt table in a dimly lit environment, holding a gold-colored smartphone and the finger sleeve. He performs a similar tapping motion, with the finger sleeve glowing red as if reacting to the phone. The narrator concludes by describing it as "a really simple, easy and amazing effect," and that "from the front view, it really does look as if you're taking a light and then throwing it back on." The video ends with a black screen displaying white text: 'PLEASE SUBSCRIBE CHECK OUT THE BOOK BELOW', all set against a backdrop of subtle, ambient instrumental music.

Later shown empty and used again in a trick where the phone is placed inside, with a "clink" sound, maintaining the illusion of emptiness. The narrator refers to it as a "glass of water that can fit behind your phone."

He introduces 'Card Magic Pro' as a pre-order for $12, arriving before Christmas, and encourages viewers to purchase it to support the channel and learn amazing magic tricks.

The Man in White Shirt refers to 'five cards the spectator chose' and 'your five cards underneath,' and later counts out '4, 3, 5, 8, 10' while arranging three cards face-up on the table, implying these are the revealed cards.

global_entity_library.Red Fabric: A crumpled red fabric, used in the magic trick to simulate pulling an object from the smartphone, appearing to emerge from the screen and later being used to complete the illusion; referred to as 'silk' by a male voice explaining the trick.
